description_long: This is a short, sturdy fence section composed of evenly spaced vertical slats connected by two horizontal rails at the top and bottom. The fence is constructed from a material that appears to be painted or coated metal, giving it a uniform, muted greenish-gray color. The top edge of the fence is flat, with each vertical slat extending to the same height, and the ends of the horizontal rails are squared off. There are no decorative elements or embellishments, resulting in a simple, utilitarian appearance suitable for urban environments such as parks, sidewalks, or residential boundaries. The structure is solid and likely designed to act as a barrier to foot traffic or to demarcate property lines. The overall build suggests durability and resistance to weathering.
description: A simple, flat-topped metal fence with evenly spaced vertical slats and two horizontal rails, finished in a muted greenish-gray color. The design is utilitarian and lacks decorative features.
description_view_0: Front view showing seven vertical slats and two horizontal rails, with a flat top and squared ends.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the fence, with vertical slats aligned in a single plane.
description_view_2: Rear view identical to the front, displaying the same arrangement of slats and rails.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the narrow profile and alignment of the slats.
